reasoning: MPC experienced a steep 12% drop in the week preceding the forecast date, closing at $167.48. This was largely driven by macro energy factors ("oil whiplash"). Short-term stock drops of this magnitude often see a slight mean-reversion ("dead-cat bounce"), making a near-term increase slightly more likely than a further drop (the YFinance model gives a 0.55 probability of being above 167.48 based on linear trend). To comply with the instructions that uncertainty should increase over time and probabilities should regress toward 0.5 for more distant horizons, I scale my probabilities down from 0.53 for the 1-week horizon to 0.52 for the 1-month and 0.51 for the 3-month horizons.
